body_txt: So, we have a Card-Carrying Villain in a G-rated show who is going to inflict a horrible torture on his victims. Most normal methods of Cold-Blooded Torture would be way too dark for something aimed at children; what can be a substitute? Tickling, of course! Being tickled is - for most people - unpleasant enough to work as an act of torture, and the censors are content that the character isn't being horribly brutalized. This is usually done with a feather on the soles of the feet (despite this not being the most effective method in Real Life ), and it is often led up to by the tickler saying " We Have Ways of Making You Talk ." Usually a subtrope of Cool and Unusual Punishment , although there are some rare serious examples. Compare Laugh Themselves Sick , which prolonged tickle torture can lead to. See also Accidental Tickle Torture for the unintentional variant, and Friendly Tickle Torture , for, well, the friendly variant, as well as Involuntary Dance for a similar unpleasant but G-rated torture.
